workflow.title: MCP Duplicate Detection
actions: Search for existing components with salvador_search_components
Check component details with salvador_get_component if found
workflow.why: Prevents creating duplicate components that already exist

workflow.title: Verify Color Contrast
actions: Use only WCAG AA compliant colors
workflow.why: Ensures accessibility compliance before writing code

workflow.title: Choose Pattern
workflow.why: Atomic design ensures consistent complexity levels

workflow.title: Implement with CVA
actions: Forward ref pattern for DOM access
workflow.why: Ensures type safety and variant consistency

requirements.tokens: Use design system tokens only (PRIMITIVES, ALIAS, SHADOWS, RADIUS)
requirements.refs: Forward ref pattern for DOM elements

requiredStories.name: AllStates
requiredStories.shows: All variants, sizes, states in organized sections

AllStates.shows: default, hover, focus, disabled, loading, error states

mobileDeviceFrames.description: Device frames for mobile-specific story testing

modalSheetPatterns.description: Responsive modal/sheet patterns for desktop vs mobile

mobile.dragHandle: Always show drag handle indicator at top

modalSheetPatterns.animationPattern: Use SheetClose for proper close animations
modalSheetPatterns.accessibilityNote: Ensure focus trap and escape key handling

stateRealMechanisms.description: Use real HTML/React mechanisms for states, not visual overrides
stateRealMechanisms.autoFocus: Use autoFocus prop for initial focus state
stateRealMechanisms.defaultChecked: Use defaultChecked for checkbox/radio initial state
stateRealMechanisms.disabled: Use disabled prop, not opacity/pointer-events classes
forbidden: Custom CSS classes mimicking disabled state

reflectRealityRule.description: Stories must reflect actual component behavior
principles: NO custom wrapper functions that modify behavior
Composition only - use component props as designed
If story needs override, component needs enhancement
reflectRealityRule.why: Stories are documentation - they must show real usage

masterStoryProtocol.purpose: Prototype master stories serve as the SOURCE OF TRUTH for page compositions and API contracts

apiSimulation.rule: Components MUST use API hooks, never inline mock data
apiSimulation.why: API hooks define the contract between frontend and backend
forbidden: Inline objects passed directly to components

compositionFidelity.rule: Stories MUST reflect the REAL page layout as it will appear in production

flowTestability.rule: User journeys MUST be completable end-to-end
examples: Login → Dashboard → Create Item → View Item → Logout

handoffGeneration.trigger: When new API types or endpoints are added

storePattern.description: API stores simulate backend state during prototype development

benefits: Frontend and backend teams share a single source of truth
API contracts are defined by actual usage, not documentation
Integration issues are discovered during development, not testing
Handoff documents are auto-generated from real code

forbidden: Skipping form inputs or action buttons

ownershipModel.description: Clear ownership of test responsibilities by layer
ownershipModel.interaction: Storybook play() for user flow testing
ownershipModel.integration: Consumer responsibility - test in consuming app context

dataTestIdByLayer.description: Test ID patterns by component layer

interactionTestPattern.description: Standard pattern for Storybook interaction tests
interactionTestPattern.imports: import { expect, within, userEvent, fn } from '@storybook/test'

example.code: play: async ({ canvasElement }) => { const canvas = within(canvasElement); const button = await canvas.findByTestId('submit-button'); await userEvent.click(button); await expect(canvas.getByText('Success')).toBeVisible(); }

jsDocTestIdPattern.description: JSDoc documentation pattern for test IDs
jsDocTestIdPattern.testIdAnnotation: @testId followed by list of all test IDs
jsDocTestIdPattern.purpose: Enables automated test ID discovery and documentation generation
